title: potentilla strigosa f. alpestris
intro: Potentilla strigosa f. alpestris, also known as alpine potentilla, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of alpine potentilla include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. Alpine potentilla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including wound healing and digestive issues.
